title: Waste Management Wonders: 3 Stocks Cleaning Up Profitably
text: While money doesn't grow on trees, it can do so on trash, thus warranting a closer examination of top waste management stocks. Basically, the sector benefits from an unavoidable reality.

text: Waste Management (WM) might move higher on growing optimism about its earnings prospects, which is reflected by its upgrade to a Zacks Rank #2 (Buy).

title: 7 Rock-Solid Dividend Stocks to Trust in Difficult Times
text: While the upcoming new year generally brings optimistic sentiment to the market, investors should be prepared for anything, which is why solid dividend stocks represent an incredibly relevant topic. Much like playing the hot corner, you've got to be ready for anything.

title: Waste Management: A Monotonous Dividend Grower That Could Help You Sleep Well At Night
text: Waste Management is a steady eddy type of business that provides a predictable stream of income for long-term dividend investors. The company is North America's leading provider of waste management services and is expected to continue growing due to the rise in waste generation. Waste Management has been rewarding shareholders with buybacks and has had a strong year in 2023, with strong projected growth for 2024.

title: 7 ‘Bulletproof' Stocks to Buy Ahead of a Wild New Year
text: Although a new year tends to bring optimism in the market, current vagaries warrant a discussion about bulletproof stocks to buy for 2024. To be fair, even the most influential voices in government are hinting at a positive outcome.

title: 7 Ever-Rising Dividend Stocks Even a Recession Can't Stop
text: Even in turbulent economic times overlayed with bear markets, recessions, and broader uncertainty, some stocks still manage to provide stability, income, and growth for investors. You should always be on the lookout for companies that can deliver rising dividends year after year.

title: Waste Management Boosts Its Dividend: What Investors Should Know
text: WM is increasing its quarterly dividend by more than 7%. The waste management company's nascent renewable natural gas business could provide a meaningful tailwind to earnings over the long haul.

title: WM Announces Plan to Increase its Dividend Rate and a Refreshed $1.5 Billion Share Repurchase Authorization
text: HOUSTON--(BUSINESS WIRE)--WM (NYSE: WM) today announced that its Board of Directors has approved a 7.1% increase in the planned quarterly dividend rate for 2024, from $0.70 to $0.75 per share. The annual dividend rate increase from $2.80 to $3.00 per share marks the twenty-first consecutive year of dividend rate increases. The Company also received authorization from its Board of Directors to repurchase up to $1.5 billion of the Company's common stock, superseding the authority remaining under.

title: EQT Infrastructure to acquire a majority position in Heritage Environmental Services, a leading provider of industrial waste management
text: Industrial waste management services constitute a vital segment of the industrial manufacturing and production process by ensuring reliable and compliant final disposal of specialized and highly regulated waste Transaction highlights EQT's commitment to partnering with purpose-driven companies that provide essential services to society. Heritage Environmental Services owns and operates 37 mission critical assets that provide the last line of defense for the environment EQT Infrastructure to accelerate Heritage's future growth trajectory with EQT's extensive experience in the environmental services sector through investments in sustainability, digitalization, and operational excellence STOCKHOLM , Dec. 11, 2023 /PRNewswire/ -- EQT is pleased to announce that the EQT Infrastructure VI fund ("EQT Infrastructure") has agreed to acquire a majority position in Heritage Environmental Services ("HES" or the "Company") from The Heritage Group.

title: Waste Management (WM) Gains 10% in 3 Months: Here's How
text: Waste Management (WM) has a consistent record of rewarding its shareholders through dividends and share repurchases.

title: Waste Management: Benefiting From Waste, Transitioning Into Clean Tech
text: Waste Management is a North American waste management company with a robust business model and unique intangible assets. The company is making sustainability investments in renewable energy and recycling, with expected potential incremental annual adjusted operating EBITDA contributions of $740 million by 2026. Waste Management reported solid financials in Q3, with increased revenue, EBITDA, and EPS, and has a strong cash position and total assets.
